Interface ProofObject<G extends @Nullable ProofGoal<G>>

All Known Implementing Classes:
InfFlowProof, Proof
public interface ProofObject<G extends @Nullable ProofGoal<G>>

A proof object provides an interface to the current proof status. It mainly manages the proof goals still to be proven.

Implementing classes might decide for a complete explicit proof object like the proof tree for a sequent calculus or just opt to a (non-proof generating) set of goals.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    add(Iterable<G> goals)
    adds a list with new goals
    boolean
    closed()
    returns true iff the specified property could be proven valid and the proof has been closed
    void
    closeGoal(G goalToClose)
    Close the given goal
    LogicServices
    getServices()
    returns the services which provide access to the meta infrastructure like namespaces for functions, sort etc.
    Iterable<? extends G>
    openGoals()
    returns an iterable collection of open goals
    void
    replace(G oldGoal, Iterable<G> newGoals)
    replace a given goal by a list of new goals

  • Method Details

    • openGoals

      Iterable<? extends G> openGoals()
      returns an iterable collection of open goals
      Returns:
      collection of ProofGoals still to be proven

    • add

      void add(Iterable<G> goals)
      adds a list with new goals
      Parameters:
      goals - the list of ProofGoals to be added

    • replace

      void replace(G oldGoal, Iterable<G> newGoals)
      replace a given goal by a list of new goals
      Parameters:
      oldGoal - the ProofGoal to be replaced
      newGoals - list of ProofGoals that replace the old goal

    • closeGoal

      void closeGoal(G goalToClose)
      Close the given goal
      Parameters:
      goalToClose - the goal to close

    • closed

      boolean closed()
      returns true iff the specified property could be proven valid and the proof has been closed
      Returns:
      true if the proof (i.e., all its goals) have been closed

    • getServices

      LogicServices getServices()
      returns the services which provide access to the meta infrastructure like namespaces for functions, sort etc.
      Returns:
      services managing the logic infrastructure
